subUnits.title: 11.1 Concept of Change of Subject of Formulae
notes: Concept of Change of Subject of Formulae
- A mathematical relationship expressed using symbols and an equals sign (=).
- Example: Area of rectangle A = l × b.
- The variable that is isolated on one side of the equals sign (usually the left).
- In A = lb, 'A' is the subject.
- Using inverse operations to isolate a different variable.
- Make x the subject: y = x + 5  →  x = y - 5.
- Make y the subject: x = 2y  →  y = x / 2.
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics change of subject formula linear site:youtube.com

quiz.q: In the formula V = IR, the subject of the formula is:
quiz.explanation: V is isolated on the left side of the equals sign.

quiz.q: If y = x + 7, making x the subject gives:
quiz.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides to isolate x.

quiz.q: If P = 2Q, making Q the subject gives:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by 2 to isolate Q.

quiz.q: Make r the subject in C = 2πr:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by 2π.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = x - 4:
quiz.explanation: Add 4 to both sides.

quiz.q: Make m the subject in y = mx + c:
quiz.explanation: Subtract c, then divide by x.

quiz.q: Make b the subject in A = 1/2 bh:
quiz.explanation: Multiply by 2, then divide by h.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in 3x = 15:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by 3.

quiz.q: Make y the subject in x + y = 10:
quiz.explanation: Subtract x from both sides.

quiz.q: Make u the subject in v = u + at:
quiz.explanation: Subtract at from both sides.

subUnits.title: 11.2 Formulae Involving Brackets and Fractions
notes: - Use the distributive law: a(b + c) = ab + ac.
- Example: Make x the subject in y = 2(x + 3).
- If y = a / x, cross-multiply to get xy = a, then x = a / y.
- Example: Make x the subject in y = (x + a) / b.
- Cross-multiply: by = x + a  →  x = by - a.
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics change of subject brackets fractions site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = 2(x + 3):
quiz.explanation: Expand to y = 2x + 6, then 2x = y - 6, so x = (y - 6)/2.

quiz.q: Make a the subject in P = 2(a + b):
quiz.explanation: Divide by 2: P/2 = a + b. Subtract b: a = P/2 - b.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = a / x:
quiz.explanation: Multiply by x: xy = a. Divide by y: x = a/y.

quiz.q: Make R the subject in V = IR:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by I.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = (x + a) / b:
quiz.explanation: Cross-multiply: by = x + a. Subtract a: x = by - a.

quiz.q: Make b the subject in A = lb:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by l.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in 2(x - 1) = y:
quiz.explanation: Divide by 2: x - 1 = y/2. Add 1: x = y/2 + 1.

quiz.q: Make y the subject in x/2 + y/3 = 1:
quiz.explanation: Multiply by 6: 3x + 2y = 6. 2y = 6 - 3x. y = 3 - 3x/2.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in a/x + b = c:
quiz.explanation: a/x = c - b. Invert both sides or cross multiply: x = a / (c - b).

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = 3(x + 2):

subUnits.title: 11.3 Formulae Involving Roots and Powers
notes: - To remove a square, take the square root of both sides.
- Example: Make r the subject in A = πr².
- To remove a square root, square both sides.
- Example: Make x the subject in y = √(x + 1).
- Example: Make v the subject in E = 1/2 mv².
diagramDescriptions: Transposition involving squares and square roots
subUnits.youtubeQuery: SS1 Mathematics change of subject square roots powers site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Make r the subject in A = πr²:
quiz.explanation: Divide by π, then take the square root.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = x²:
quiz.explanation: Take the square root of both sides.

quiz.q: Make l the subject in T = 2π√(l/g):
quiz.explanation: T/2π = √(l/g). Square both sides: (T/2π)² = l/g. Multiply by g.

quiz.q: Make v the subject in E = 1/2 mv²:
quiz.explanation: 2E = mv². v² = 2E/m. v = √(2E/m).

quiz.q: Make r the subject in V = 4/3 πr³:
quiz.explanation: 3V/4π = r³. Take the cube root.

quiz.q: Make x the subject in y = √(x + 1):
quiz.explanation: Square both sides: y² = x + 1. Subtract 1: x = y² - 1.

quiz.q: Make a the subject in v² = u² + 2as:
quiz.explanation: Subtract u², then divide by 2s.

quiz.q: Make l the subject in A = l × w:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by w.

quiz.q: Make r the subject in C = 2πr:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by 2π.

quiz.q: Make h the subject in V = πr²h:
quiz.explanation: Divide both sides by πr².
